last_completed_item: D-110 — Stable baseline thruster capacity with rare access to at most one additional charge
current_item: D-111 — Base locomotion acceleration and inertia between thruster bursts
next_action: Ask exactly one concise A/B/C decision card selecting whether ordinary mech movement uses immediate tight velocity changes, momentum-heavy inertial movement, or a responsive hybrid with quick acceleration and bounded drift. Preserve the signature chainable directional thruster burst, movement-input boost direction independent from aim, limited boost steering, predictable wall ricochets, weapon-specific movement effects, keyboard and gamepad controls, later Android controls, broad difficulty accessibility, deterministic speedrunning, and future co-op. Include aiming stability, dodge precision, movement satisfaction, controller and touch feel, interaction with recoil and ricochets, level-design implications, accessibility, tuning complexity, and the risk of base movement making the thruster either redundant or mandatory. Put the agent recommendation after all A/B/C options, at the end of the card. Queue accepted decisions D-111 through D-120 and persist the complete batch at D-120 before asking D-121, unless a context handoff or explicit user instruction requires earlier persistence.
